title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a man giving information to people who are going to help in a community garden.
audio_script: Man: Hello, and thank you for offering to help at the community garden this spring. We are very pleased because the garden has become more popular each year. First of all, please remember that new volunteers should meet at the back entrance next to the [14]library[/14]. The front gate is only for visitors during public opening hours. Our first working morning is on [15]Saturday[/15], not Sunday as some people were told last month. We will start at nine thirty, but please come ten minutes earlier so that we can give you gloves and tools. Most of the jobs are simple. Some people will plant flowers, while others will clean the paths. The most important job this week is repairing the wooden [16]fence[/16] around the vegetable area. It was damaged by wind during the winter. At break time we will provide tea, coffee, and biscuits in the [17]kitchen[/17]. You are welcome to bring your own lunch, especially if you are staying for the afternoon. Please wear strong shoes, because the ground can be wet. You should also bring a [18]hat[/18] if it is sunny, as there is not much shade. Finally, the garden manager's phone number is on your information sheet, but for quick questions today you can ask [19]Laura[/19], who is standing near the blue shed. If you cannot stay for the whole morning, please tell one of the regular volunteers before you leave. We need to know who is still on site for safety reasons. Photos may be taken for our newsletter, but let us know if you prefer not to be included. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.

The speaker says the first working morning is on Saturday.
The speaker says the most important job is repairing the wooden fence.
The speaker says tea, coffee, and biscuits will be provided in the kitchen.
